steps.vulnerability_name: Add bounds to number_of_repeats in serializer
steps.description: Enforce min/max on number_of_repeats at the input validation layer so no integer outside 1-10 can reach business logic

The manual bounds check only fired when repeat_request_if_failed=True, leaving number_of_repeats unchecked on the other path. Now that the serializer enforces bounds unconditionally, remove the redundant and incomplete guard.

verification.description: Request with number_of_repeats=999 must be rejected with HTTP 400 — serializer bounds enforcement
verification.curl: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://167.99.136.89:8888/identity/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"user@example.com","password":"Passw0rd!"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token',''))"); curl -s -w '\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}' -X POST http://167.99.136.89:8888/workshop/api/merchant/contact_mechanic -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"mechanic_api":"http://127.0.0.1","repeat_request_if_failed":true,"number_of_repeats":999}'

verification.description: Request with number_of_repeats=0 must also be rejected — min_value=1 enforced
verification.curl: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://167.99.136.89:8888/identity/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"user@example.com","password":"Passw0rd!"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token',''))"); curl -s -w '\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}' -X POST http://167.99.136.89:8888/workshop/api/merchant/contact_mechanic -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"mechanic_api":"http://127.0.0.1","repeat_request_if_failed":true,"number_of_repeats":0}'
